title: Voluntary Provisioning
definition: A unique form of predation in which a host actively offers itself as food to the predator, acknowledging the inevitable relationship and negotiating terms through generosity.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Imagine a situation where a creature offers food willingly to its predator. This is an unusual act of submission that recognizes the predator-prey dynamic and attempts to set conditions for feeding, rather than trying to avoid or resist.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: A form of predation in which the host voluntarily provides itself as food. Unlike traditional predation methods such as appeasement or deterrence, this behavior acknowledges the inevitability of predation and re-frames the host's role through generosity, creating an iterative negotiation dynamic.
difficulty_levels.expert: This concept engages with the anthropology of ritual, game theory, and the philosophy of agency under constraint. The cookie offering functions as a costly signal in a repeated asymmetric game: the host demonstrates (1) acceptance of the feeding arrangement, (2) resource sufficiency (a depleted host cannot afford to offer food), and (3) psychological stability. The predator, receiving this signal, can reduce feeding frequency or intensity — a 'generous tit-for-tat' dynamic that stabilizes the relationship. The ritual transforms predation from a zero-sum encounter into an iterated negotiation.
